UNLAWFUL DETAINER MOTION 501, DEFENDANT GREGORY POTTER'S MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION IS DENIED. NO NEW FACTS, LAW OR CIRCUMSTANCES PRESENTED. PER GREGORY POTTER DECLARATION 3 AND 7, BRIAN TARPANIAN VACATED THE PREMISES ON SEPTEMBER 1, 2013, I.E. 26 DAYS BEFORE THE HEARING ON MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT. DEFENDANT ALSO FAILS TO EXPLAIN HOW THIS "NEW" CIRCUMSTANCE SUPPORT HIS ARGUMENT THAT DEFAULT WAS ENTERED DUE TO MISTAKE, SURPRISE OR EXCUSABLE NEGLECT. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER APPROVED AS TO FORM. JUDGE RONALD EVANS QUIDACHAY, CLERK: M. OLOPERNES, NOT REPORTED

UNLAWFUL DETAINER MOTION 501, DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT IS DENIED. DEFENDANT FAILED TO PRESENT COMPETENT EVIDENCE TO REBUT PRESUMPTION OF PROPER SERVICE. ADDITIONALLY, DEFENDANT CONCEDES THAT HE HAD NOTICE OF A LAWSUIT FILED AGAINST HIM. DEFENDANT FAILS TO SUSTAIN BURDEN BOTH UNDER CCP 473.5 AND CCP 473(B). CCP 425.11 IS NOT APPLICABLE. calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-07-2014 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-03-2013 Continued To Jan-22-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Noice Of Motion And Moiton For Request For Court Judgment By Default
Off Calendar Dec-05-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Dept. 514, Judge: Donald Sullivan, Clerk: Yolanda Mazariegos, CSR: Sonia Wong, #11288.

Plaintiff An Ho Peng appearing on his own behalf.
Selby Lighthill, Esq., appearing for Defendant Gregory Potter.

Default of Defendant(s) has been duly entered.

Plaintiff An Ho Peng sworn and examined on his own behalf.
After Evidence and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiff and against Defaulted Defendant(s) as follows:

1. This Court declares that Defendant Gregory A. Potter sublet a portion of the premises at 272 Cotter Street San Francisco, CA 94112 without prior written consent of the owner constituted a breach of the rental agreement between Plaintiff, An Ho Peng and Defendant Gregory A. Potter.
2. This Court grants Injunctive Relief and orders Defendant, Gregory A. Potter, not to sublet any portion of the premises at 272 Cotter Street, San Francisco, CA 94112 without written consent of the owner.
3. The defendant Gregory A. Potter shall provide required information and documents about any proposed subtenant to Plaintiff according to the Rules and Regulation of San Francisco Rent Stabilization and Arbitration Board 6.15 B (b)(1)(i) to (vii).
4. Plaintiff, An Ho Peng, shall recover the cost of suit against Defendant Gregory A. Potter in the amount of Eight hundred thirty five ($835.00) Dollars.
Court signed Judgment in open court.

Noice Of Motion And Moiton For Request For Court Judgment By Default
Off Calendar Nov-14-2013 Continued To Dec-05-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 514
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Dept. 514, Judge: Donald Sullivan, Clerk: Yolanda Mazariegos, CSR: Sonia Wong, #11288.

Plaintiff An Ho Pen appearing on his own behalf.
Shelby Lighthill, Esq., appearing for defendant Gregory Potter.

After argument of Plaintiff and counsel for Defendant the court ordered matter continued to 12/5/2013 at 9:00 AM, in Department 514. Plaintiff to file and serve responsive brief on or before November 21, 2013. Respondent to file and serve reply brief on or before November 27, 2013. Courtesy copies to be delivered directly to Department 514. The Court ordered Plaintiff and Counsel for defendant to meet and confer try to resolve this matter after further briefs have been submitted. Parties to meet and confer between the period of November 27, 2013 and December 5, 2013.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-28-2013 Continued To Dec-18-13 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Reconsideration
Off Calendar Nov-12-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Selby Lighthill, Esq., Attorney at Law, (415-771-6174), for Gregory Potter, Defendant and moving party.
An Ho Peng, Plaintiff in pro per, (415-215-9345), opposing party.

ARGUED. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT GREGORY POTTER's Motion For Reconsideration is DENIED. No new facts, law or circumstances presented. Per Gregory Potter Declaration 3 and 7, Brian Tarpanian vacated the premises on September 1, 2013, i.e. 26 days before the hearing on Motion to Set Aside Default. Defendant also fails to explain how this "new" circumstance support his argument that Default was entered due to mistake, surprise or excusable neglect. Prevailing party to prepare order approved as to form. Judge Ronald Evans Quidachay, Clerk: M. Olopernes, Not reported (D501)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-23-2013 Continued To Nov-13-13 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Defendant'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Default
Off Calendar Sep-27-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Selby Lighthill, Esq. of Law Offices of Selby Lighthill, (415-771-6174), for Gregory A. Potter, Defendant and moving party.
An Ho Peng, Plaintiff in pro per and opposing party, (415-215-9345).

ARGUED. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows: Defendant's Motion To Set Aside Default is DENIED. Defendant failed to present competent evidence to rebut presumption of proper service. Additionally, Defendant concedes that he had notice of a lawsuit filed against him. Defendant fails to sustain burden both under CCP 473.5 and CCP 473(b). CCP 425.11 is not applicable. Order is signed in open court. Judge Ronald Evans Quidachay, Clerk: M. Olopernes, Not reported (D501)

MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
Off Calendar Sep-27-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
NO APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING:

DEFENDANT GREGORY POTTER'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND is transferred to be heard in Department 501 on the same date (September 27, 2013) at 9:30 a.m. That department handles all landlord-tenant matters. Judge: Leslie C. Nichols, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185. =(302/LCN)

MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
Off Calendar Sep-11-2013 Continued To Sep-27-13 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT GREGORY POTTER's MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND.
is continued to September 27, 2013 to give moving party the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. Moving party must deliver courtesy copies of the moving papers to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than September 11, 2013 at 4:00 pm or the motion will be denied. The Court has copies of the opposition papers. Any exhibit must be separated by an exhibit tab per CRC 3.1110(f). Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =(302/MJM)
